items.title: Enhance cross-site user identity handling in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a cross-site user identity guard to ensure users are directed to the correct account when navigating between AceDataCloud sub-sites. It adds a `user_id` parameter to outbound links, which is evaluated by the destination site's router guard to manage user sessions effectively. The implementation includes new utility functions and router modifications to enhance user experience and security.

items.title: Enhance Cross-Site User Identity Management in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a cross-site user ID guard to ensure users are correctly authenticated when navigating between AceDataCloud sub-sites. It adds a new utility for appending the current user ID to outbound links, preventing confusion when users are logged into different accounts. The router guard has been integrated to handle user ID validation, enhancing the overall user experience and security.

items.title: Enhance cross-site user experience with identity guard in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a cross-site user identity guard to ensure users are directed to the correct account when navigating between AceDataCloud sub-sites. Key changes include the addition of a utility for annotating outbound links with the current user ID and a router guard to handle incoming links. This enhancement improves user experience by preventing confusion caused by mismatched sessions across different sites.

items.title: Fix logo height on OAuth callback page in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where the AceData logo was displayed at an incorrect size on the connector OAuth callback page. By adding a height constraint to the logo component, it now matches the sizing used in other callback pages, ensuring a consistent appearance across connectors. This change impacts all connectors that share the same callback structure, enhancing the user interface.

items.title: Expanded Domain Coverage for AutoCert in Auto Cert 🚀
items.summary: This update adds `*.studio.acedata.cloud` to the AutoCert domain list for wildcard certificate issuance. The `renew.py` script and `README.md` have been updated accordingly to reflect this change. The new domain will require a manual trigger of GitHub Actions post-merge for it to take effect.

items.title: Update Nexior Consumer App URL in Index 🚀
items.summary: The documentation for the Nexior consumer app has been updated to reflect its new primary URL, now accessible at https://studio.acedata.cloud/. This change is documented in the `AGENTS.md` file, which also includes a symlink to `CLAUDE.md`. Both the old and new URLs will continue to serve the same Nexior bundle for the time being.

items.title: Update fallback origin to studio.acedata.cloud in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the SSR/initial-paint fallback origin in the router's WebApplication JSON-LD schema, changing it from 'https://hub.acedata.cloud' to 'https://studio.acedata.cloud'. This change only affects scenarios where the window location is unavailable, ensuring that the actual hostname is still used during real visits. Both hostnames will continue to serve the same Nexior bundle.

items.title: Update Chinese Docs to Reflect New Studio URL in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces all instances of `hub.acedata.cloud` with `studio.acedata.cloud` in the Chinese documentation located in `docs/zh-CN/`. Key changes include modifications across 25 files, ensuring consistency in branding. Additionally, the publish script has been updated to point to the new consumer URL, facilitating a smoother documentation pipeline.

items.title: Update CTAs to Open Studio in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the public roadmap CTAs to direct users to the new Studio Platform at studio.acedata.cloud. Key changes include updates to the top navigation, hero section, and footer buttons, ensuring a seamless transition from the previous Hub links. The README has also been updated to reflect these changes.

items.title: Update Links to New Studio Domain in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies user-facing links in the PlatformFrontend to point to the new Studio Platform domain, `https://studio.acedata.cloud`. Key changes include updates to the 'Try Now' menu item, footer links, and SEO JSON-LD data. This ensures a seamless transition for users while maintaining compatibility with existing links.

items.title: Update sidebar to reflect Studio Platform in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: The sidebar entry previously labeled 'UX Platform' has been renamed to 'Studio Platform' and now directs users to studio.acedata.cloud. This change includes updates to constants, utility functions, and localization files across 18 languages, ensuring a consistent user experience. The new link is already live and serves the same Nexior bundle.

items.title: Add missing i18n keys for better localization in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update adds 13 missing static i18n keys across various components, including API, headshots, QR art, site, and suno. The changes ensure that users see the correct translated labels instead of raw key strings, enhancing the overall user experience. The new keys have been added to both English and Chinese locale files, with other languages to be updated in the next translation cycle.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces automatic updates for internationalization (i18n) translations based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include updates to console.json files across multiple languages, enhancing the user experience for non-Chinese speakers. The changes ensure that the platform remains accessible and user-friendly for a diverse audience.

items.title: Fix download button localization in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update adds the missing localization for the download button in the chat entity card, ensuring it displays the correct translated labels in both English and Chinese. The key 'common.button.download' has been defined in the respective language files, resolving the issue where the raw key was shown instead of the intended text. After deployment, users will see 'Download' or '下载' as expected.

items.title: Fix chat URL navigation issue in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a bug where the chat URL did not update correctly when switching conversations, leading to issues with page refresh and navigation. The fix involves replacing params-only router pushes with path-based pushes, ensuring the URL reflects the current conversation. This change enhances user experience by maintaining accurate navigation paths across different chat models.

items.title: Enhance Console with OAuth Links in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces three new external links in the Console's left sidebar, directing users to OAuth Apps, Connectors, and Skills pages on auth.acedata.cloud. These additions are strategically placed under the User Profile section for better organization and accessibility. The i18n files for English and Chinese have been updated to reflect these changes.

items.title: Polish chat UI with icon alignment and button restoration in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update addresses three UI regressions in the chat message row, enhancing the overall professionalism of the interface. Key changes include aligning the edit and copy icons, ensuring the assistant's avatar and answer text are vertically aligned, and restoring the copy and regenerate buttons for all non-empty responses. These improvements contribute to a more cohesive and user-friendly chat experience.

items.title: Fix GitHub SSO client_id fallback for smoother logins in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the GitHub SSO client_id fallback to align with the historical OAuth App credentials. The previous configuration caused invalid redirect URI errors due to mismatched client IDs. Now, the frontend is back in sync with the production settings, ensuring a seamless login experience for users.

items.title: Streamline chat toolbar by removing skill and connector buttons in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the skill and connector entry buttons from the top-right toolbar of the chat conversation page. The changes simplify the interface by eliminating links that opened separate tabs for skills and connections, while leaving other functionalities intact. No additional translations or icon registrations were affected by this modification.

items.title: Reverting GitHub SSO Client ID for Better User Experience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update rolls back the GitHub App migration due to issues with token scopes and user re-authorization requirements. The classic OAuth App tokens will continue to be used, ensuring a smoother experience as they do not expire. Additionally, the refresh button remains hidden to maintain the intended user interface.

items.title: Reverted GitHubAdapter to Classic OAuth App Behavior in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update rolls back the GitHub App migration, restoring the classic OAuth App credentials for the K8s 'oauth-github' Secret. Key changes include the reversion of token handling, ensuring existing users are unaffected while new authorizations correctly populate scopes. Additionally, the refresh button feature has been adjusted to enhance user experience by hiding it when not applicable.

items.title: Streamline Skills Modal by Removing Sidebar in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The left sidebar in the Skills page's Browse Skills modal has been removed to enhance user experience. This change eliminates unnecessary space and focuses on the active 'Skills' tab, allowing the main content area to utilize the full width of the dialog. Unused CSS selectors related to the sidebar have also been deleted, simplifying the codebase.

items.title: Enhance Shadow Portfolio Reporting in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `solradar shadow-portfolio` CLI, which generates a markdown report from existing JSONL files, providing a clear view of counterfactual P&L for paper trades. The report is automatically updated with each cron cycle, allowing users to track performance without manual calculations. Additionally, new tests ensure the reliability of the report generation process.

items.title: Enhance Browse Skills with Installed Badges in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces visual indicators for installed skills in the Browse Skills dialog, enhancing user experience. Cards now display a green checkmark for installed items, while installable items show a '+' sign. Additionally, a soft sync mechanism has been implemented to handle installation conflicts gracefully.

items.title: Enhance Skill Catalog with Installed Flag in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an 'installed' flag for each skill in the catalog, allowing the UI to accurately display whether a user has installed a skill. A new indexed field, `catalog_identifier`, tracks the original identifier of catalog items, ensuring correct identification even after renames. The implementation maintains efficient database queries, ensuring performance remains optimal.

items.title: Enhance GitHub App Token Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of requested scopes for GitHub App user-to-server tokens, which previously returned empty scopes. By carrying resolved scopes through the signed state, the system now correctly populates Tool Permissions on the connections page, even when the upstream token response omits them. Existing connections will need to be re-authorized to reflect the updated scopes.

items.title: Streamlined DeepSeek Model Names in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update simplifies the display names of DeepSeek models in the chat model picker for better consistency. The labels have been changed to a uniform short form, enhancing clarity without affecting the underlying model names or functionality. Only the English and Chinese localization files were modified, ensuring a smooth user experience.

items.title: Enhance chat input handling for IME users in Nexior ✨
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where pressing Enter while using a CJK IME would unintentionally submit the chat message. The fix involves modifying the key event handling in both the main chat input and the message edit textarea to prevent submission during IME composition. Users can now confirm their IME candidates without sending messages prematurely.

items.title: Enhanced Logging for GitHub OAuth in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces detailed logging for the GitHub OAuth process, improving error traceability during login failures. Key changes include enhanced exception handling in the AuthViewSet and better logging of API responses in the GitHubAdapter. Sensitive information is now redacted in logs, ensuring security while maintaining visibility into issues.

items.title: Fix GitHub SSO 500 Error Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical 500 error during GitHub SSO login by improving error handling when the GitHub App lacks the 'Email addresses' permission. The changes include normalizing the response from the `/user/emails` endpoint and implementing defensive checks in the login process to prevent crashes. These enhancements ensure a smoother user experience and maintain system stability.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated translation updates for internationalization (i18n) based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include updates to various language files, enhancing the localization experience across multiple languages. The changes aim to improve user accessibility and engagement with the application.

items.title: Fix GitHub SSO client_id for seamless login in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue with GitHub SSO login in production. The client_id in the configuration file has been updated to match the new GitHub App, resolving the 500 error during login attempts. This change ensures users can authenticate smoothly without encountering errors.

items.title: fix connection deletion response in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The DELETE endpoint for connections now correctly returns a 204 No Content status instead of a 500 Internal Server Error. This change resolves a pre-existing bug where the response was not properly handled after a successful deletion. The fix involved moving the return statement to ensure the correct response is sent back to the frontend.

items.title: Fix chat composer dropdown issue in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update resolves a bug where the composer dropdown in the chat interface was unresponsive when clicking the '+' button. The fix involves wrapping the tooltip in a dedicated trigger element to ensure proper functionality of the dropdown. After the change, the dropdown now opens correctly, displaying the options for 'Upload from files', 'Skills', and 'Connectors'.

items.title: Enhance Skill Catalog with Composio Source and Auto-Sync in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ComposioHQ curated source, adding 28 new community skills to the catalog, bringing the total to approximately 45. A new CronJob has been implemented to automatically sync the skill catalog every 6 hours, ensuring up-to-date skills are available. Additionally, improvements to the matching logic allow for better handling of different directory structures.

items.title: Boosted OpenAI Checker with Parallelism and Priority Sorting in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant enhancements to the OpenAI checker by implementing parallel processing for account checks and prioritizing capabilities based on their urgency. The new structure allows for up to 32 concurrent HTTP probes, drastically reducing the time for a full pass from minutes to approximately 10 seconds. Additionally, capabilities are now sorted to ensure that the most critical checks are performed first, improving the overall efficiency and responsiveness of the system.

items.title: Enhance GitHubAdapter for Refreshable Tokens in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enables the GitHubAdapter to handle refreshable tokens after transitioning to a GitHub App. Key changes include modifying the adapter to support token rotation and implementing a refresh mechanism that works seamlessly with existing connections. The migration ensures that users retain their identities while benefiting from improved token management.

items.title: Remove Redundant Permission Buttons in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update eliminates the non-functional allow/ask/deny buttons from the user connections page, as they lacked backend support and had no impact on the system. The changes streamline the UI by removing unnecessary elements and related code, resulting in a cleaner user experience. The update involves modifications to a single file, reducing the codebase significantly.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Checker Scheduling for Better Coverage in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the OpenAI checker CronJob configuration to improve its efficiency and coverage. By changing the concurrency policy to 'Forbid' and adjusting the schedule to every 5 minutes, the checker can now complete a full pass without interruption, ensuring that all capabilities are validated in a timely manner. This change significantly reduces the risk of outdated capabilities being marked as valid, enhancing overall system reliability.

items.title: Enhance Runtime Model Fallback for Resilient Responses in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a runtime model fallback chain that activates when all providers for a requested model fail. By refactoring the account retrieval and request handling processes, the system can now successfully return responses from fallback models instead of a generic error. This improvement enhances user experience by ensuring more reliable access to model services even during provider outages.

items.title: Clean Up Dead Code in Nexior 🧹
items.summary: This update removes unused code related to the chatConversationV2 method and associated types, which were remnants from an earlier prototype. The changes include the deletion of 197 lines of code across two files, streamlining the chat operator functionality. The active chatConversation method remains intact, ensuring continued support for the current production entry point.

items.title: Enhance error handling for upstream failures in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a mechanism to treat specific upstream account-side 4xx errors as retryable, preventing sensitive billing messages from being exposed to end users. Key changes include the addition of a soft-failure detection method and improved sanitization of error responses. The implementation ensures that users receive a generic error message instead of detailed upstream errors, enhancing user experience and security.

items.title: Enhance Skills Discovery with New Browse Skills Dialog in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Browse Skills' dialog that allows users to explore a directory of skills, enhancing the previous functionality that did not provide any useful interaction. The dialog features a responsive grid layout, search functionality, and a streamlined installation process for skills. Key files have been added and modified to support this new feature, improving user experience significantly.

items.title: Launch of Public Skill Catalog in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a public skill catalog that allows users to browse curated skills from upstream sources, enhancing the user experience significantly. Key features include a new model for catalog items, REST endpoints for fetching and installing skills, and a management command for syncing skills from external repositories. The implementation ensures that users can easily discover and install skills without duplicating storage.

items.title: Hide Refresh Button for Unsupported Providers in Frontend Web 🚫
items.summary: This update hides the Refresh button for GitHub connections that do not support refresh tokens, addressing a user experience issue. The change is implemented by adding a new 'supports_refresh' flag to the connection interface, allowing the UI to conditionally render the button. Other connections, such as Google, remain unaffected.

items.title: Enhance Connection Serializer with Refresh Support Flag in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new boolean field, **supports_refresh**, in the Connection serializer to indicate whether a connection can be refreshed. This change prevents confusion for users by hiding the Refresh button for providers that do not support refresh tokens, such as GitHub OAuth Apps. The implementation is read-only and does not alter existing database behavior.

items.title: Enhance Chat with Streaming Reasoning Block in Nexior 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Thinking...' block that streams reasoning from models like DeepSeek-R1 and Claude 4, enhancing user interaction in the chat interface. The block auto-collapses to a 'Thought process' toggle once the model's answer is provided, ensuring a seamless experience. Key components have been rewritten to support this feature, including updates to the conversation handling and internationalization.

items.title: Enhance Model Reasoning Visibility in Platform Service 🤖
items.summary: This update improves the aichat2 worker's handling of reasoning content by ensuring that all reasoning-capable models can surface their thinking processes. Key changes include the introduction of a new ThinkTagSplitter to manage in-band reasoning tags and automatic enabling of extended thinking for Claude models. These enhancements will allow Nexior to render a 'Thinking...' block effectively, improving user experience.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates for Multiple Languages in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translation updates from the Chinese (zh-CN) source. Key modifications include enhancements across various language files, ensuring better localization support for users. The changes aim to improve user experience by providing accurate translations in multiple languages.

items.title: Streamline Grok Module by Removing Unsupported Models in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update simplifies the Grok module in Nexior by removing unsupported models: `grok-3-reasoning` and `grok-3-deepsearch`. Key changes include the deletion of constants and references in multiple files, ensuring that only valid models are available for selection. This enhances the user experience by preventing routing requests to non-billable models.

items.title: Optimize Vuex Module Loading in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces lazy loading for per-app Vuex modules, significantly reducing the entry chunk size from 717 KB to 626 KB. The new lazy registration ensures that only the necessary modules are loaded when users navigate to specific routes, improving performance and user experience. Additionally, persisted state is maintained across sessions, enhancing the application's reliability.

items.title: Streamline Initial Load by Removing PSL and Lazy-Loading FingerprintJS in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the initial load performance of Nexior by eliminating the `psl` dependency and implementing lazy-loading for `FingerprintJS`. The entry chunk size has been significantly reduced from 206 KB to 142 KB gzipped, contributing to a total reduction of approximately 700 KB in initial download size. Key changes include an inline implementation for domain resolution and deferring fingerprint initialization to improve critical path execution.

items.title: Introducing a Stunning 404 Page in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a beautifully designed 404 error page that enhances user experience by providing a clear call-to-action. Users will now see a vibrant, branded not-found page with animated backgrounds and options to navigate back home or return to the previous page. The catch-all route ensures that any unknown URL leads to this informative page instead of a blank screen.

items.title: Add DeepSeek V4 Flash to Chat Model Selector in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the DeepSeek V4 Flash model to the Nexior chat model selector, enhancing user options. Key changes include updates to the constants and localization files, ensuring that users can now select V4 in the consumer app. The backend is already configured, and the model is now visible in the chat interface.

items.title: Upgrade to GPT-5.5 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the new default chat model, GPT-5.5, while removing the outdated GPT-5.4-nano. The ChatGPT group now supports models GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, and GPT-5.4-mini, ensuring users have access to the latest technology. All necessary backend support is already in place, making this change effective immediately upon deployment.

items.title: Enhance chat composer with popover actions and improved previews in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the chat composer actions into a single popover menu, streamlining the user interface. File previews are now displayed inline above the textarea, resolving previous overlap issues with the toolbar. Additionally, raw JSON tool-call blobs are now rendered correctly in the assistant's text, improving readability and user experience.

items.title: Streamline initial load by optimizing element-plus chunking in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the manual chunking rule for `element-plus`, allowing Rollup to automatically split components into smaller chunks. As a result, the initial download size is reduced by approximately 156 KB-gz, improving load times significantly. The critical path now only includes essential components, while others are fetched lazily as needed.

items.title: Enhance chat with inline entity cards in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces inline rendering of rich-output entity cards in chat messages, replacing the previous flat URL-only display for media artifacts. The new `EntityCard` component supports various types including audio, video, images, and files, enhancing user interaction and experience. Backwards compatibility is maintained, ensuring older deployments function as expected without disruption.

items.title: Enhance Media Interaction with Rich Entity Cards in Platform Service 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces rich entity cards using the `<acard>` tag, allowing the aichat2 assistant to present media artifacts like audio and video as interactive components. The new card parser processes these tags in real-time, ensuring a seamless user experience while maintaining backwards compatibility. Additionally, the system prompt has been restructured into Liquid templates for better management and scalability.

items.title: Streamline PR Workflow for Agents in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update reorders the pull request workflow for agents, allowing users to create a PR before running heavy local validations. By surfacing the PR URL immediately, users can start reviewing while tests run in the background. The changes also clarify the workflow steps to prevent reverting to the old process.

items.title: Organize Worktrees for a Cleaner Workspace in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the organization of worktrees by placing them in a dedicated `.worktrees/` directory at the workspace root. This change prevents clutter in the main workspace and ensures that worktrees do not appear in the `git status` output. The documentation has been updated to reflect this new structure and explicitly advises against the previous sibling-of-submodule placement.

items.title: Enhance Parallel Sessions Workflow in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new section in AGENTS.md detailing the 'Parallel Sessions & Git Worktrees' workflow. It establishes guidelines for managing concurrent AI threads working on the same submodule, emphasizing the importance of using worktrees to prevent cross-session bugs. Additionally, the PR workflow has been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring a smoother development process.

items.title: Enhance Chat Experience with URL Routing and Lazy Loading in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the URL bug in the side-panel and implements URL-driven conversation routing, allowing for scenario-specific conversation management. Key changes include lazy-loading message history and improved conversation fetching, enhancing user experience with instant access to previously opened conversations. The side-panel has been cleaned up to rely on server-side filtering, streamlining the conversation display.

items.title: Enhance Conversations List with Model Group Filtering in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update splits the `/conversations` list endpoint into two phases, allowing for faster loading by filtering rows based on `model_group`. Key changes include the introduction of a `model_group` field, a new summary endpoint that returns conversation previews without messages, and the implementation of MongoDB indexes for efficient querying. A backfill script is also provided to populate the `model_group` for existing conversations.

items.title: Optimize Load Time by Dropping Vendor-Web3 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update significantly reduces the initial load size by removing the vendor-web3 chunk, which previously included all Web3-related packages. Key changes include lazy loading of the Solana Web3 module and making third-party requests non-blocking, resulting in a lighter and faster application. The overall eager JS and CSS size has decreased from approximately 3.9 MB to 1.8 MB, enhancing user experience on first paint.

items.title: Enhance Playback Options with Speed Adjustment and Prompt Reuse in Nexior 🎶
items.summary: This update introduces two key features: the 'Adjust Speed' action and the 'Reuse Prompt' functionality. Users can now adjust playback speed with segmented choices and easily reuse prompts from previous tasks, enhancing the editing experience. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for both English and Chinese languages.

items.title: Automated research cycle update in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new report covering 19 candidates, including 8 new listings, 2 trending from GeckoTerminal, and 9 from the watchlist. Key outcomes include 1 hold on GME, 9 skips due to various red flags, and no buys meeting the score threshold. The report is based on multiple data sources, ensuring comprehensive analysis.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automated translation updates for the i18n files based on changes from the zh-CN source. All relevant cache files have been renamed to reflect these updates, ensuring that the localization remains current and accurate. This enhancement will streamline the localization process and improve user experience for Chinese-speaking users.

items.title: Fix auto-sync for package.json before publish in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where the publish workflow was failing due to a version mismatch between the local package.json and the npm registry. The package.json version has been bumped to 3.35.0, and a new auto-recovery step has been added to ensure future discrepancies are automatically corrected. This change will streamline the publishing process and prevent similar issues from arising in the future.

items.title: Enhance CI with BOT_GITHUB_TOKEN in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the translate workflow to use the BOT_GITHUB_TOKEN instead of the default GITHUB_TOKEN. This change ensures that auto-translate PRs will trigger CI checks and can be auto-merged once they pass. The previously stuck PR will be unblocked through a close and reopen process using the bot's PAT.

items.title: Upgrade GitHub Release Action to v3 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the softprops/action-gh-release from version 2 to 3, transitioning the action runtime from Node 20 to Node 24. The change ensures compatibility with the latest Node environment, enhancing performance and security. The workflow file has been updated accordingly to reflect this new version.
